_readme.what_this_file_is: Domain-specific vocabulary exceptions for DSCL. Words listed here are permitted in model output even if they fall outside the primary and fallback vocabulary lists.
_readme.when_to_add_words: Add a word here when it is legitimate and necessary for a specific domain but would otherwise be flagged as out-of-vocabulary. Example: 'jurisdiction' is correct in legal_contract but would be unusual in a plain_summary.
_readme.global_key: Words under 'global' are permitted in all domains regardless of context. Use sparingly — only for words that are universally legitimate regardless of audience or register.
_readme.adding_new_domains: To add a new domain, create a new key matching the domain name exactly as it appears in domain_register.json, then list the permitted words as a JSON array of lowercase strings.
_readme.note: This file is intentionally customisable. If you are using DSCL in a specialised field — legal, medical, technical, scientific — extend the relevant domain list or add a new domain key to match your use case.
